name: ci
description: Diagnoses and fixes CI/CD pipeline failures. Use when user mentions 'CI', 'GitHub Actions', 'GitLab CI', 'ビルドエラー', 'テスト失敗', 'パイプライン', 'CIが落ちた', or asks to analyze build/test failures. Do NOT load for: ローカルビルド, 通常の実装作業, レビュー, セットアップ.
capabilities: Analyzes CI/CD failure logs from GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
Identifies error patterns including TypeScript, npm, and Jest errors
Provides step-by-step fix instructions for failing tests
Routes between analysis and fix workflows based on user intent
Includes error pattern dictionaries for quick reference
author: Chachamaru127
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with no executable code. The 82 static findings are all false positives triggered by the scanner misinterpreting markdown code blocks as executable Ruby/shell commands. The code fences (`bash`, `typescript`, `markdown`) in documentation are legitimate examples, not actual command execution. A previous audit confirmed this skill uses only Read/Grep/Bash tools within repository scope.
valueStatement: CI/CD pipelines fail frequently and debugging takes time. This skill analyzes failure logs, identifies root causes, and provides specific fix recommendations for build and test errors.
limitations: Cannot access private CI systems without gh CLI authentication
Cannot modify CI configuration files directly
Cannot guarantee fix success for complex multi-file issues
Requires user approval for test file modifications

useCases.title: Analyze CI failure logs
useCases.description: Extract and interpret GitHub Actions logs to identify root causes of build failures

useCases.title: Fix failing tests
useCases.description: Diagnose why tests fail and receive specific guidance on correcting test or implementation code

useCases.title: Understand build errors
useCases.description: Get clear explanations of TypeScript, npm, and dependency errors with fix recommendations

promptTemplates.title: Check recent failures
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the recent CI runs and why the latest one failed

promptTemplates.title: Analyze error logs
promptTemplates.prompt: Look at the GitHub Actions log and tell me why the build is failing

promptTemplates.title: Fix test failures
promptTemplates.prompt: The CI test is failing. Find the failing test and suggest how to fix it

promptTemplates.title: Full CI diagnosis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the CI failure, identify the root cause, and provide a specific fix for the issue

bestPractices: Always verify the root cause before applying fixes to avoid masking real bugs
Run tests locally with npm test before committing changes
Check recent commits to understand what changed when CI starts failing
antiPatterns: Skipping tests with it.skip to make CI pass
Removing assertions instead of fixing the implementation
Bypassing CI checks with continue-on-error settings

faq.question: What CI systems are supported?
faq.answer: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and any CI system accessible via gh CLI or log files.

faq.question: Can this skill fix complex multi-file issues?
faq.answer: For complex cases with 2+ retry cycles, it delegates to the ci-cd-fixer sub-agent for deeper analysis.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: It provides fix guidance. Direct edits require your explicit approval before execution.

faq.question: Is my CI data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. All analysis happens locally. Logs are read through the gh CLI within your authenticated context.

faq.question: What if the fix does not work?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes failure reasons first. If the initial fix fails, it escalates to a sub-agent for deeper diagnosis.

faq.question: How is this different from general debugging?
faq.answer: This skill is specialized for CI-specific patterns including TypeScript errors, npm failures, and test timeout scenarios.
